encounter.reason: Diabetes, CKD, and neuropathy worsening during caregiving stress

labs.name: estimated glomerular filtration rate
labs.note: value documented as about 38 mL/min/1.73 m2

labs.name: low-density lipoprotein cholesterol
labs.interpretation: borderline / above ideal for diabetes and CKD

screening.name: PHQ-2 depression screening
screening.interpretation: positive brief depression screen with grief/stress symptoms discussed; patient denied self-harm thoughts

conditions.name: type 2 diabetes mellitus with hyperglycemia
conditions.note: glycemic control documented as worsening / poorly controlled with A1c 9.4% and fasting glucose 214 mg/dL in the setting of missed meals and inconsistent medication timing

conditions.name: diabetic peripheral neuropathy of both feet
conditions.note: worsening burning foot pain, increased toe numbness, and objective sensory loss on foot exam led to treatment escalation

conditions.name: diabetic kidney disease
conditions.note: significant albuminuria documented with urine albumin-creatinine ratio 342 mg/g and worsening renal reserve influencing medication decisions

conditions.name: chronic kidney disease stage 3b
conditions.note: CKD stage 3b was explicitly documented by trend with creatinine 1.56 mg/dL and eGFR about 38 mL/min/1.73 m2

conditions.name: hypertension
conditions.note: blood pressure remained above goal at 154/92 mmHg, increasing concern in the setting of diabetes and CKD

conditions.name: hyperlipidemia
conditions.note: LDL remained 92 mg/dL and statin therapy was renewed without a new diagnosis or dose change

conditions.name: heart failure with reduced ejection fraction
conditions.note: no direct status change documented during this office visit

conditions.name: paroxysmal atrial fibrillation
conditions.note: no direct rhythm or anticoagulation change documented during this office visit

conditions.name: caregiver stress / grief reaction
conditions.note: psychosocial stress and grief symptoms were documented, PHQ-2 was 2/6, and patient denied self-harm thoughts

medications.name: metformin oral medication
medications.reason: type 2 diabetes mellitus with hyperglycemia and worsening renal function / gastrointestinal intolerance

medications.name: insulin glargine 24 units subcutaneous nightly
medications.sig: inject 24 units nightly and increase by 2 units every 3 nights until fasting glucose is under 140 mg/dL

medications.name: empagliflozin 10 mg oral tablet daily
medications.sig: take 10 mg by mouth once daily
medications.reason: type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic kidney disease and heart-failure benefit

medications.name: gabapentin 300 mg oral capsule nightly
medications.sig: take 300 mg by mouth at bedtime for burning neuropathic foot pain

medications.name: atorvastatin 40 mg oral tablet nightly
medications.reason: hyperlipidemia / cardiovascular risk reduction in diabetes and CKD

document_references.description: outpatient primary care follow-up note documenting worsening diabetes control, CKD stage 3b with albuminuria, worsening diabetic neuropathy, hypertension above goal, and caregiving-related stress. The note should summarize symptoms, vital signs, foot exam findings, reviewed laboratory results, medication changes, counseling, follow-up planning, and referral rationale.

medication and self-management instructions covering metformin discontinuation, insulin glargine titration steps, gabapentin precautions, daily foot checks, skin care, regular meals, medication adherence, stress effects on glucose, and return precautions. The document should also note planned repeat labs in about 3 months and possible disruption from the upcoming move.

focused foot exam record describing diminished monofilament sensation, reduced vibration sense, callus formation, and absence of ulcers bilaterally. The document should support diabetic neuropathy reassessment and foot-care counseling.

diagnostic_reports.description: LDL cholesterol was reviewed at 92 mg/dL, but the note does not provide a credible set of lipid panel components
hemoglobin was reviewed at 12.1 g/dL, but the note does not provide enough individual hematology components to support a complete blood count report-with-results

diagnostic_reports.type: Repeat diabetes and renal monitoring labs planned in about 3 months
diagnostic_reports.description: follow-up laboratory plan includes repeat hemoglobin A1c, repeat metabolic / renal function testing, and repeat urine albumin assessment; no future results available yet
